item.title: 20 nice and wholesome things to brighten your day, vol 200
item.description:
A woman’s voice activates the same part of a man’s brain as hearing music does.
I barbecue often, and when I do I always bring in the best bit I can to my wife; regardless of how late at night it is. It’s gotten to the point where she loves me barbecuing, because she knows delicious treats are close at hand.

item.dc:creator: Konelli

item.title: I’ve been a nurse for 8 years and a fantasy nerd my whole life… so I combined them.
item.description: Two years ago... on an oncology unit, during one of those long shifts that makes you rethink everything, I had this weird thought: What if nurses had magic? I’ve loved fantasy since I was a kid. Dragons, curses, destiny, all of it. But I also love medicine. The science. The quiet heroism. So I started writing a series where healers use magic… but still chart, still struggle, still face burnout and impossible choices. I’ve been working on it ever since. I just released Book 2 and somehow... I’m up to 28 reviews on Amazon and doing okay on Goodreads too. It’s tiny in the big publishing world, but it feels huge to me. Anyway. Just wanted to share a small milestone with people who understand weird passion projects, a small win with the internet that raised me. Overall journey: 5/7. Perfect score. If you’ve ever mashed two parts of yourself together into something new, I’d love to hear about it.
item.dc:creator: SageSalven

item.title: Dump of 16 things I recently found satisfying. Enjoy, have a nice day !
item.description:
Two wheel car thing. But Why ?
Babies' sleeping positions defy the limits of physics.
Gyotaku began centuries ago as a way for Japanese fishermen to record fish size and species, eventually evolving into beautiful traditional art.
Carving Chess Pieces on an Automated Lathe.
A Folding ladder. Why not ?
Hmmm .... Interesting.
This curling shot ...
It is very peaceful to watch.
How low can you go ? His next version
Perfectly fitting sheetrock on stairs. Already seen here, but still so amazing.
A smooth niche curve.
A gentleman demonstrating some impressive driving skills.
Fast painting. Results can be smoother because of the continuous intake.
It's time to wrap it up! Wow those guys can really do it. One more reason to be helped by Professionals.
Gluing together whatever the hell this is. But in the end we see what it is, and it's not a fish.
He could have saved the Titanic.
item.dc:creator: TerribleBot

item.title: Manipulating AI for fame and fortune
item.description:
A week ago, BBC tech reporter Thomas Germain wrote an article explaining how he "hacked" ChatGPT and Google to get the AI engines to believe he was a hot dog eating champion. Full article here: https://www.bbc.com/future/article/20260218-i-hacked-chatgpt-and-googles-ai-and-it-only-took-20-minutes
Now, I do my best to avoid AI (I have an extension that blocks the AI summaries in Google), but I wondered if I could make this trick work for me. I figured, since I'm a writer with a far smaller audience than Germain and less of a concrete internet presence, I might be able to go even further with it. So I wrote this blog post with over a dozen "facts" about me: https://10cities10years.com/2026/02/23/who-is-joseph-lyttleton/
After posting this yesterday, I checked Google and Bing results. After 15 minutes, their AI summary of me still contained the same basic info that had always been there. But, a couple hours later, I checked again, and it had worked. This is a screenshot of a DuckDuckGo search for me (which, as I understand it, uses Bing's AI):
Furthermore, this is a screenshot of Google's AI results, which includes my favorite "fact" about me:
Will these algorithms eventually figure out this is all crap and correct? I have no idea. But for now, I'm Joseph Lyttleton, the only self-published novelist to be nominated for the Booker and Pulitzer Prizes, and honestly, it's an honor just to be nominated. #fuckAI My award-nominated novels: The Awakening: https://www.amazon.com/Awakening-Joseph-Lyttleton-ebook/dp/B0G2CDSZ7W/ref=tmm_kin_swatch_0 Yahweh's Children: https://www.amazon.com/Yahwehs-Children-Joseph-Lyttleton-ebook/dp/B078RDB9P4
item.dc:creator: mortaleye

item.title: I have that going for me I guess.
item.description: Over the last few years have worked hard not to keep bad people in my life. To not force people to stay if they don't want to. Breakups are especially hard for me... been dating a guy since August. He broke up with me tonight. I didn't do my usual breakdown and beg and plead and then freak the fuck out and have a break down like I used to years ago. I teared up but said okay I'll go then. Had a good cry with my best friend and now laughing at Family Guy. Small growth you know?
item.dc:creator: BlessedBeTheBruised

item.title: Anyone have source to the full clip?
item.description: Social sciences on display here: Violation of consent: verbally, physically, this woman is actively saying NO. Observational learning: the two gits at the bar need their man cards revoked. Whether they are learning pickup artist techniques, or if the ended up learning that there are consequences to being a violent assaulter (dark shirt guy), they and all of us are learning through social observation. Percussive resocialization: socialization can happen from our family, friends, teachers, peers. Once out in the world interacting with adult agency, resocialization is correcting our behavior by others. This is done in forms of endorsements and sanctions. Usually, extreme adverse behavior that has been unsantioned for long periods of time receives higher velocity, high impact resocializations. Don't be those two at the bar. Don't be the dark shirt guy thinking she's playing hard to get. Don't keep your head down and wait till it all blows over. Be better
item.dc:creator: theSoulciologist

Suggested Track Playlists: (HIT SHUFFLE) YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLoIzKPbKfwniKOaadw-tB8kaEQiUQ1Yp4 Spotify: https://open.spotify.com/playlist/6cqV52puia81vNnPHxcz1W?si=yoUknxpQQVql9E-NZRQ4 ICM SQUAD: @DerpyVaz, @ohvalleyofplenty, @nichoDriver, @Dirgekins, @TheForbiddenSemicolon, @aRootinaTootin, @Gorgoror, @kaeldra, @MooreBeers, @cloverleafbane, @Crapmonster ICM TIP JAR: https://www.patreon.com/InterwebChexMix Please note that we do not claim ownership of any random art, comics, or photos featured in this collection; we only take credit for the daily effort that goes into compiling the collections themselves. Like what we do and how we do it? Leave a tip. Or don't. It's totally cool either way. Stay True & Stay Tuned - Meta Pathos
item.dc:creator: MetaPathos
